title: /_ping handler does not respond with "OSType" header as expected by Docker client
body:
### Issue Description

Docker client attempts to ping a remote server (e.g. in case of Mac) to obtain various information about the server.

 https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/client/ping.go#L57

However, `/_ping` in Podman Machine does not return the OSType header
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/compat/ping.go#L15

### Steps to reproduce the issue

`docker run` complains about an unknown server OS (empty string) when supplying a `--device=...` flag.

```
$ docker run --device=/dev/fuse nginx
docker: unknown server OS: .
See 'docker run --help'.
```




### Describe the results you received

`OsType` is missing from the Podman socket response

```
$ curl -sI --unix-socket ~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman.sock http:/v1.41/_ping
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Api-Version: 1.41
Builder-Version:
Buildkit-Version:
Cache-Control: no-cache
Docker-Experimental: true
Libpod-Api-Version: 4.6.1
Libpod-Buildah-Version: 1.31.2
Pragma: no-cache
Server: Libpod/4.6.1 (linux)
X-Reference-Id: 0xc0000155a8
Date: Mon, 28 Aug 2023 06:07:17 GMT
```

### Describe the results you expected

In comparison, Colima returns the `Ostype` header
```
$ curl -sI --unix-socket ~/.colima/default/docker.sock http:/v1.41/_ping
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Api-Version: 1.41
Builder-Version: 2
Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate
Content-Length: 0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
Docker-Experimental: false
Ostype: linux
Pragma: no-cache
Server: Docker/20.10.20 (linux)
Date: Mon, 28 Aug 2023 06:07:26 GMT
```

Seems undocumented ?

https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.43/#tag/System/operation/SystemPingHead

Part of the Windows API.
